DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF BIANCA SIMBALL'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES BY DEFENDANT ABM INDUSTRIES, INC. TO PLAINTIFFS FIRST SET OF REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS; AND MONETARY SANCTION REQUEST AGAINST DEFENDANT AND/OR ITS ATTORNEY OF RECORD - PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S REPLY DECLARATION IS IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 2015.5. DEFENDANT'S LATE-FILED OPPOSITION ALSO WAS ACCEPTED AND CONSIDERED. THE REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION WERE TIMELY SERVED ON DEFENDANT AND THE TIME FOR RESPONDING EXPIRED BEFORE DEFENDANT SERVED RESPONSES. DEFENDANT RECEIVED TWO EXTENSIONS TO SERVE RESPONSES, WHICH PLAINTIFF GRANTED, MAKING RESPONSES DUE ON OCTOBER 29. HOWEVER, THE PROOF OF SERVICE REFLECTS SERVICE BY MAIL ON OCTOBER 30, WHICH IS LATE AND THEREFORE WAIVES OBJECTIONS UNDER CCP 2031.010. MOREOVER, THE RESPONSES INCLUDED BOILER-PLATE OBJECTIONS AND IT APPEARS THAT NOT ALL RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS WERE PRODUCED. VERIFICATIONS WERE NOT RECEIVED UNTIL NOVEMBER 11. ADDITIONAL MEET AND CONFER EFFORTS PROVED FRUITLESS. ACCORDINGLY, DEFENDANT SHALL SERVE AMENDED, VERIFIED RESPONSES WITH DOCUMENTS BY MAY 30, 2016, WITHOUT OBJECTIONS EXCEPT PRIVILEGE. IF ANY INFORMATION IS WITHHELD ON PRIVILEGE GROUNDS, THEN A SUFFICIENTLY DETAILED PRIVILEGE LOG MUST BE PROVIDED WITH THE RESPONSES. THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM: AARON MINNIS, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED. =302/JPT

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF BIANCA SIMBALL'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES BY DEFENDANT ABM INDUSTRIES, INC. TO PLAINTIFFS FIRST SET OF SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES; AND MONETARY SANCTION REQUEST AGAINST DEFENDANT AND/OR ITS ATTORNEY OF RECORD - THE MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S REPLY DECLARATION IS IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 2015.5. DEFENDANT'S LATE-FILED OPPOSITION IS ALSO CONSIDERED. THE SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES WERE TIMELY SERVED ON DEFENDANT AND THE TIME FOR RESPONDING EXPIRED BEFORE DEFENDANT SERVED RESPONSES. DEFENDANT'S RESPONSES WERE LATE, WAIVING OBJECTIONS PER CCP 2030.290(A). SUBSEQUENT MEET AND CONFER EFFORTS PROVED FRUITLESS AND LED TO AMENDED RESPONSES THAT APPEAR INCONSISTENT AND/OR EVASIVE. ACCORDINGLY, DEFENDANT SHALL PROVIDE VERIFIED ANSWERS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS. 10, 19, 21, 22, AND 32, THAT ARE AS COMPLETE AND STRAIGHTFORWARD AS THE INFORMATION REASONABLE AVAILABLE TO DEFENDANT PERMITS, WITHOUT OBJECTIONS BY MAY 30, 2016. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-29-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JILL COHOE OF MANNING & KASS LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-415-217-6990 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS ABM INDUSTRIES INC., ABM ONSITE SERVICES, INC. AND ABM ONSITE SERVICES WEST, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT - The ABM defendants' motion for summary judgment is granted. The ABM defendants satisfied their burden of production that they are not the legal cause of plaintiff's injuries and plaintiff failed to submit any evidence creating a triable issue of material fact. The undisputed facts show that the ABM defendants did not breach a duty of care and did not have actual or constructive notice of the dropped tray prior to the accident. At page 42 of her deposition, plaintiff testified that the tray slid out so quickly that she could not avoid stepping on it. That admission demonstrates that, even if ABM defendants owed a duty to maintain the corridor, they did not breach that duty as a matter of law because they did not have sufficient time to remedy the problem. The admission also shows that there was insufficient time for the moving defendants to have constructive notice of the dropped tray. There is also no evidence of actual notice of the dropped tray. "[W]here the plaintiff relies on the failure to correct a dangerous condition to prove the owner's negligence, the plaintiff has the burden of showing that the owner had notice of the defect in sufficient time to correct it." (Ortega v. Kmart Corp. (2001) 26 Cal.4th 1200, 1206.) In response to defendants' facts 12-13, plaintiff admits that defendants' employees were not the only employees who carried trays in the corridor. Plaintiff further admits that the identity of the person who dropped the tray is unclear. (Plaintiff's Depo., 44:6-11; 49:11-18.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF BIANCA SIMBALL's Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant Abm Industries, Inc. To Plaintiffs First Set Of Special Interrogatories; And Monetary Sanction Request Against Defendant And/Or Its Attorney Of Record - The motion to compel responses to special interrogatories is GRANTED. Plaintiff's reply declaration is in compliance with CCP 2015.5. Defendant's late-filed opposition is also considered. The special interrogatories were timely served on defendant and the time for responding expired before defendant served responses. Defendant's responses were late, waiving objections per CCP 2030.290(a). Subsequent meet and confer efforts proved fruitless and led to amended responses that appear inconsistent and/or evasive. Accordingly, defendant shall provide verified answers to special interrogatories Nos. 10, 19, 21, 22, and 32, that are as complete and straightforward as the information reasonable available to defendant permits, without objections by May 30, 2016. The request for sanctions is denied. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro tem: Aaron Minnis, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not Reported. =302/jpt

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant Abm Industries, Inc. To Plaintiffs First Set Of Requests For Production Of Documents; And Monetary Sanction Request Against Defendant And/Or Its Attorney Of Record
Off Calendar Apr-29-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ROGER L. MEREDITH, TEL. NO. 1-415-616-5959 EXT. 115 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF BIANCA SIMBALL's Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant Abm Industries, Inc. To Plaintiffs First Set Of Requests For Production Of Documents; And Monetary Sanction Request Against Defendant And/Or Its Attorney Of Record - Plaintiff's motion to compel responses to production of documents is GRANTED. Plaintiff's reply declaration is in compliance with CCP 2015.5. Defendant's late-filed opposition also was accepted and considered. The requests for production were timely served on defendant and the time for responding expired before defendant served responses. Defendant received two extensions to serve responses, which plaintiff granted, making responses due on October 29. However, the proof of service reflects service by mail on October 30, which is late and therefore waives objections under CCP 2031.010. Moreover, the responses included boiler-plate objections and it appears that not all responsive documents were produced. Verifications were not received until November 11. Additional meet and confer efforts proved fruitless. Accordingly, defendant shall serve amended, verified responses with documents by May 30, 2016, without objections except privilege. If any information is withheld on privilege grounds, then a sufficiently detailed privilege log must be provided with the responses. The request for sanctions is denied. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro tem: Aaron Minnis, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not Reported. =302/jpt
